title: payment — PHP attribute contracts
description: Three class-level attributes. `#[AsPaymentListener]` subscribes a service to payment-lane events (intent lifecycle + capture + dispute + circuit-breaker). `#[AsPaymentProvider]` marks a class as a provider driver — the PaymentProviderManager discovers these at boot and registers them via createXxxDriver factories. `#[AsPaymentWebhookHandler]` marks a class as a per-provider webhook handler; the payment.webhook_verify middleware dispatches inbound webhooks to the matching handler.

attributes.name: AsPaymentListener
attributes.description: Marks a service as a subscriber to payment-lane events (PaymentIntent* / Payment* / PaymentMethod* / PaymentDispute* / PaymentProvider*). Discovery loader picks up each class at boot + wires the declared events. Consumers of critical events (PaymentDisputeLost, PaymentProviderCircuitBreakerOpened) should override queue to `payment-critical`.

params.name: events
params.description: Array of event short names (`PaymentCaptured`, `PaymentDisputeOpened`, `PaymentProviderCircuitBreakerOpened`, …). Empty = subscribe to every `Stackra\Finance\Payment\Events\*` event. Names resolve against events.json at boot; typos abort boot with a discovery error.

params.name: queue
params.description: Optional queue override. Falls back to the event's default queue (see events.json).

params.name: priority
params.description: Handler ordering within a single event. Lower runs first. Atomic side-effects (MarkInvoicePaidOnPaymentCaptured priority 90 → RecordTransactionOnPaymentCaptured priority 80) declare lower priorities to run before non-atomic side-effects.

attributes.name: AsPaymentProvider
attributes.description: Marks a class as a payment provider driver. The PaymentProviderManager discovers these at boot and registers a createXxxDriver factory keyed on the slug. Every driver MUST implement the IPaymentProvider contract (name(), createIntent, confirmIntent, captureIntent, cancelIntent, attachPaymentMethod, detachPaymentMethod, listIntents, submitDisputeEvidence, verifyWebhookSignature) + ship a JSON Schema under data/providers/<slug>-config.schema.json for config validation + a matching webhook handler decorated with #[AsPaymentWebhookHandler].

params.name: slug
params.description: Provider slug matching the PaymentProvider enum (stripe, paddle, adyen, square, paypal, custom_webhook). One driver class per slug.

params.name: supported_method_types
params.description: PaymentMethodType values this provider can dispatch: card / bank_debit / bank_transfer / wallet / installment / crypto / cash / check. Read at boot into data/provider-capability-matrix.json (source of truth). Observer enforces new PaymentMethods reference a provider that supports the requested method_type.

params.name: supports_3ds2
params.description: true = the provider supports 3DS2 challenge flow. Stripe: true. Adyen: true. Square: true (via Square 3DS API). PayPal: true (via Braintree 3DS). Paddle: true (native support in Paddle Billing). CustomWebhook: caller-defined (typically false unless the caller implements 3DS2 themselves).

params.name: supports_manual_capture
params.description: true = the provider supports authorization holds (capture_method='manual'). All 6 providers support this day-1.

params.name: supports_saved_methods
params.description: true = the provider supports tokenized saved methods (SetupIntent-equivalent flow). All 6 providers support this day-1.

params.name: supports_marketplaces
params.description: true = the provider supports multi-party payments (marketplace splits between the platform + sub-merchants). Adyen: true. Stripe: true (via Stripe Connect). Square: true. Others: false day-1.

params.name: supports_webhook_verification
params.description: true = the provider signs webhooks with a shared secret verifiable via HMAC-SHA256 (or provider-specific scheme like PayPal certificate verification). Every day-1 provider supports webhook verification.

attributes.name: AsPaymentWebhookHandler
attributes.description: Marks a class as a per-provider webhook handler. The payment.webhook_verify middleware dispatches inbound webhooks to the matching handler after signature verification. Every provider ships a handler that translates provider-native events into our internal state transitions.

params.name: provider
params.description: Provider slug matching the PaymentProvider enum. One handler class per provider.

params.name: handled_event_types
params.description: Provider-native event types this handler processes. Stripe: ['payment_intent.succeeded', 'payment_intent.payment_failed', 'charge.dispute.created', 'charge.dispute.closed', 'payment_method.detached', ...]. Paddle: ['transaction.completed', 'transaction.canceled', ...]. Boot-time discovery emits a coverage report — unhandled webhook types fail-soft with a warning.
